comments.body:
I checked the linked PR comment plus the broader issue chain. The concrete answer is: this needs **two layers**.

1. **Repo-local onboarding baseline** from PR #62.
   That PR is still open, and current `main` does **not** have `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml`, `.agent/src/onboarding.ts`, or the trigger-label constants/tests. Merge or recreate those first. That gives us an idempotent `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup` workflow that can create labels and the `Sepo setup check` issue.

2. **Hosted GitHub App installation bootstrap**.
   This cannot be done by repo-local Actions alone. GitHub App installation events are delivered to the App webhook URL, not to workflows in the newly installed repo. The current repo has workflow/auth helpers, but no hosted app webhook service.

Concrete current-repo changes I’d make:

- Add/land PR #62 onboarding files:
  - `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml`
  - `.agent/src/onboarding.ts`
  - `.agent/src/cli/onboarding-check.ts`
  - `.agent/src/trigger-labels.ts`
  - `.agent/src/__tests__/onboarding-check-cli.test.ts`
  - docs updates in `README.md`, `.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md`, `.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`

- Add a reusable bootstrap module/CLI for the hosted app path, for example:
  - `.agent/src/installation-bootstrap.ts`
  - `.agent/src/cli/installation-bootstrap.ts`
  - `.agent/src/__tests__/installation-bootstrap.test.ts`

  This should:
  - accept `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `INSTALLATION_ID`, `DEFAULT_BRANCH`, and a GitHub token
  - check for `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml`
  - call existing `dispatchWorkflow()` from [`github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts#L224) to dispatch `agent-onboarding.yml`
  - if dispatch fails, create/update one fallback `Sepo setup check` issue using existing issue/comment helpers in [`github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts#L47)
  - use a hidden marker like `<!-- sepo-agent-installation-bootstrap -->` for idempotency

- Add docs for the app-install path:
  - update [`using-your-own-github-app.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/using-your-own-github-app.md)
  - update [`setup-guide.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/setup-guide.md)
  - update quick start to say: installing the Sepo App will try automatic onboarding, but provider secrets still need owner setup

- Add/confirm app permission docs:
  - `Actions: write` for workflow dispatch
  - `Issues: write` for setup issue/comment fallback
  - `Metadata: read` for repository metadata
  - current docs already mention `Actions` and `Issues` in [`using-your-own-github-app.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/using-your-own-github-app.md#L10)

Needed outside this repo:

- Add a hosted webhook endpoint for the Sepo GitHub App, likely in the existing hosted service behind `self-evolving.app` / the OIDC broker.
- It must receive `installation` and `installation_repositories` events, verify `X-Hub-Signature-256`, mint an installation token, then call the new bootstrap logic.
- If that hosted service code lives in another repo, this repo can only add the repo-side workflow/helper/docs, not the actual webhook receiver.

Execution plan before coding:

1. Merge or recreate PR #62’s onboarding workflow and CLI in this branch.
2. Add `installation-bootstrap` helper with dispatch-first, issue-fallback behavior.
3. Add tests with fake `gh` calls covering workflow-present, workflow-missing, dispatch-failure, and idempotent issue update.
4. Update deployment docs with the exact automatic-install behavior and limitations.
5. Run `npm --prefix .agent test` and YAML parsing.

I’d proceed with the repo-local side first. The only blocker is where the hosted GitHub App webhook code actually lives; if it is not in this repository, the endpoint implementation needs a separate change in that service.

@sepo-agent /implement 

Can you create a follow up pr stacked on top of the existing onboarding pr?

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-119/codex-25286562888`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/120

Stacked this follow-up on PR #62 and added a repo-side installation bootstrap CLI/module for hosted or self-managed GitHub App webhooks. It dispatches the onboarding workflow when available, otherwise creates or updates one marked `Sepo setup check` fallback issue; docs now describe the automatic install path and provider-secret limitation. Verified with build, focused bootstrap/onboarding CLI tests, and workflow YAML parsing; the full local suite was attempted but stale ignored `.agent/dist` artifacts from another branch caused obsolete dist-only tests to run.

title: App installation automation
